CONTENT.Honours: Yokohama F. MarinosJ1 League: 2022Japanese Super Cup: 2023Servette FCSwiss Cup: 2023–24JapanEAFF Championship: 2022
CONTENT.Club career: On 31 August 2018, he signed a 4-year contract with the Russian Premier League club CSKA Moscow. He scored his first Russian Premier League goal for CSKA on 9 March 2019 in a 3–0 victory over FC Rubin Kazan.On 10 January 2020 CSKA announced that he would join Portuguese club Portimonense on loan for the rest of the 2019–20 season, with a purchase option. On 26 March, Nishimura's loan deal at Portimonense was ended, prematurely, to allow Nishimura to return to Vegalta Sendai on loan until June 2020.On 11 February 2021, Vegalta Sendai announced that he would return to the club on a permanent transfer.In 2022, he moved to Yokohama F. Marinos and scored a goal to win the team in the league title.On 11 February 2023, he scored the winning goal at the 2023 Japanese Super Cup, giving Marinos their first Japanese Super Cup title.On 10 February 2024, he joined Servette on loan with an option to buy This option to buy was not activated by the club and he left the club when his loan deal expired on the 30th June 2024, as a Swiss Cup champion.

SUMMARY: Takuma Nishimura (西村 拓真, Nishimura Takuma, born October 22, 1996) is a Japanese football player. He plays for Swiss club Servette on loan from Yokohama F. Marinos, and the Japan national team.
